Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Nov-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS CORE40 HAYES VALLEY, LLC., CORE40 SOMA, LLC, CORE40 CASTRO, LLC'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT.

Appearances: Laura Reathaford and Caroline Sayers of Lathrop Gage LLP (310.789.4617) appearing via Court Call for defendants and moving parties; No other appearances.

This matter is not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants' motion to file cross-complaint is granted. California law strongly favors leave for such a filing. (Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 99.) Filed weeks after a key deposition and 10 months before trial, the motion is timely. What plaintiffs say is "bad faith" are factual disputes. If the cross-complaint is "legally insufficient," as plaintiffs argue, they may file a demurrer. Order signed. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600 (Tel: 415.516.8416, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com)
